issuesFound.title: :focus-visible removes focus outline without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator. This removes focus rings for keyboard users, making it difficult or impossible for them to identify focused elements. Users with low vision or cognitive disabilities are particularly affected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 317: Add a visible focus style, e.g., box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); or outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary);

issuesFound.title: :focus:not(:focus-visible) removes focus outline without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: This rule removes the focus outline for mouse users without providing a visible alternative. While intended to suppress focus rings on mouse click, it can also remove focus indicators for programmatic focus or certain assistive technology interactions, harming keyboard and screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 321: Remove this rule or add a visible focus style for mouse focus scenarios.

issuesFound.title: .btn:focus-visible removes focus outline without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Buttons are critical interactive elements; missing focus indicators prevent keyboard users from knowing which button is focused, breaking navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 400: Add a visible focus style, e.g., box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); or outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary);

issuesFound.title: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible removes focus outline without replacement

issuesFound.fix: Line 484: Add a visible focus style, e.g., box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); or outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary);

issuesFound.title: .nav-toggle:focus-visible removes focus outline without replacement

issuesFound.title: #nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible removes focus outline without replacement

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on mobile navigation trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open() and close() functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen‑reader users will not be notified of the menu state change, breaking the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2014: In the open() function, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after line 2014. Line 2024: In the close() function, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after line 2024.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on dropdown trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show/hide dropdown panels but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Without this, screen‑reader users cannot know whether the dropdown is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2129: In _openInstance(), add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after line 2129. Line 2143: In _closeInstance(), add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after line 2143.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed for billing period switch
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle button (data-billing-toggle) changes between monthly and annual billing but does not set aria-pressed. Screen‑reader users cannot perceive the toggle state (pressed/unpressed).
issuesFound.fix: Line 3048: In _syncToggleState(), after `toggleClass(_toggleTrack, CSS.checked, isYear);` add `if (_toggleEl) setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', isYear);`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic filter results not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the updated product count and empty state are displayed visually but not announced via a live region. Screen‑reader users are not informed of the content change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2504: In applyFilters(), after `_updateEmptyState(totalMatches);` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) : `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic price change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period toggle changes prices, the new prices are displayed but no live region announcement is made. Screen‑reader users may not notice the price update.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3017: In setPeriod(), after `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });` add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error does not set aria-invalid on invalid field
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator _renderResult() function marks invalid fields with a CSS class and error message but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input. Screen‑reader users may not be alerted that the field is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3574: In the else block of _renderResult(), after `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);` add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. Also, in the valid block, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialized on mobile navigation trigger at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded set initially. Screen‑reader users will not know the initial collapsed state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1990: In mobileNav init(), after `_initialised = true;` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialized on dropdown triggers at page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded set initially. Screen‑reader users will not know the initial collapsed state of dropdown panels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2100: In dropdownMenu init(), after registering each dropdown, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation does not manage focus when opening
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu opens, focus is not moved into the menu. Keyboard users remain trapped outside the menu, breaking focus management for modal dialogs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2014: In open(), after adding the open class, add `if (_drawer) { const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus(); }`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation does not return focus to trigger on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place on the page, causing disorientation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2024: In close(), before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });` add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown does not return focus to trigger on close
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown closes via Escape or outside click, focus is not returned to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place, breaking the expected focus flow.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2143: In _closeInstance(), add `if (returnFocus) inst.trigger.focus();` after the emit call.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden mobile navigation drawer not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu closes, the drawer is hidden with CSS but aria-hidden is not set to true. Screen‑reader users may still navigate into the hidden drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2024: In close(), after removing the open class, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. In open(), add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden dropdown panel not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel closes, it is hidden with CSS but aria-hidden is not set to true. Screen‑reader users may still interact with the hidden panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2143: In _closeInstance(), after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` add `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. In _openInstance(), add `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Click event listener on non-button element without keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle element (data-billing-toggle) has a click event listener but no keydown handler for Enter/Space. If the element is not a native button, keyboard users cannot activate the toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3048: In init(), after `_toggleEl.addEventListener('click', _onToggleClick);` add `_toggleEl.addEventListener('keydown', _onToggleKeydown);` and define _onToggleKeydown to handle Enter and Space keys.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update via innerHTML without live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer updates the listbox content via innerHTML but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technologies may not know that search suggestions have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1845: In render(), after setting innerHTML, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.searchResultsFound ? STRINGS.announce.searchResultsFound(items.length, query) : `${items.length} suggestions available`);`.

issuesFound.title: Auto‑advancing carousel without pause/stop control for keyboard users
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonials carousel auto‑advances with setTimeout but does not provide a keyboard‑accessible pause/stop mechanism. Users who need more time to read content cannot pause the rotation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3280: In init(), ensure the play/pause button is keyboard‑accessible and has an aria-label that changes between "Pause automatic slide rotation" and "Start automatic slide rotation". Also, add a keyboard event listener to pause on Space/Enter when the carousel has focus.

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not clear aria-invalid on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the validation function removes the error class but does not clear aria-invalid (or set it to false). Screen‑reader users may continue to hear an invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3574: In the valid block of _renderResult(), after `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);` add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: aria-controls missing on mobile navigation trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer's id. Screen‑reader users cannot perceive which element the button controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1990: In mobileNav init(), after `_drawer = qs(SELECTORS.navDrawer || SELECTORS.primaryNav);` add `if (_trigger && _drawer) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'));`.

issuesFound.title: aria-controls missing on dropdown triggers
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons do not have aria-controls pointing to their associated panel's id. Screen‑reader users cannot understand the relationship between trigger and panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2100: In dropdownMenu register(), after `const panelId = srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'dropdown-panel');` add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically shown/hidden scroll‑to‑top button not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll‑to‑top button is shown/hidden based on scroll position but aria-hidden is not toggled accordingly. Screen‑reader users may encounter a hidden button that is still focusable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2250: In _update(), when setting `_btn.removeAttribute('hidden');` add `setAttr(_btn, 'aria-hidden', 'false');`. Line 2258: When setting `_btn.setAttribute('hidden', '');` add `setAttr(_btn, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open and close functions do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen-reader users will not be informed of the menu's expanded/collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2003: add `setExpanded(_trigger, true);` after `_open = true;`. Line 2019: add `setExpanded(_trigger, false);` after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not set initial aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded initialized on page load. Screen-reader users will not know the initial collapsed state of the menu, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In the init function, after `_trigger` is assigned, add `setExpanded(_trigger, false);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle switch (role="switch") does not update aria-checked when toggled. Screen-reader users cannot perceive the current state (monthly/annual), violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In the _syncToggleState function, after `toggleClass(_toggleTrack, CSS.checked, isYear);`, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', isYear);`.

issuesFound.title: form validation does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator marks fields as invalid but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input elements. Screen-reader users will not be alerted to the validation error, violating WCAG 3.3.1 (Error Identification).
issuesFound.fix: In the _renderResult function, in the else block (when invalid), add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. In the if block (when valid), add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not manage aria-hidden on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is hidden visually but not programmatically hidden from screen readers when closed. This can cause screen-reader users to encounter hidden content, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2003: add `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`. Line 2019: add `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: product filter updates are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the visible product cards but does not announce the change to screen-reader users. Users who rely on assistive technologies may not know the filter results have updated, violating WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).
issuesFound.fix: In the applyFilters function, after emitting the 'filter:applied' event, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) : 'Filter applied');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu init does not set initial aria-expanded on triggers
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons are not initialized with aria-expanded="false" on page load. Screen-reader users will not know the menus are initially collapsed, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In the register function, after setting aria-controls, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: testimonials carousel play/pause button does not have aria-pressed initialized
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel play/pause button does not have aria-pressed set initially. Screen-reader users cannot perceive the initial paused state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In the init function, after setting up the play button, add `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'false');` if auto-play is disabled, or `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'true');` if auto-play is enabled.

issuesFound.title: scroll-to-top button visibility not reflected in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button is hidden with the hidden attribute but does not have aria-hidden set when hidden. This may cause inconsistent behavior with some assistive technologies, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2125: add `_btn.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` after `_btn.removeAttribute('hidden');`. Line 2135: add `_btn.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` inside the setTimeout callback after `_btn.setAttribute('hidden', '');`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function changes the billing period and updates prices but does not announce the change to screen-reader users. Users who rely on assistive technologies may not know the prices have updated, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: In the setPeriod function, after updating the prices, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing');` or `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing');` based on the period.

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` function changes the visible state of the navigation drawer but does not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the menu has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1144: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing._syncToggleState` function updates the visual state of the billing period buttons but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2148: add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter._updateCountBadge` function updates the product count text, but there is no accompanying write to a live region. Screen reader users are not notified when the number of filtered results changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1766: add `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`);`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function applies CSS classes and error text but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field when validation fails.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1415: add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function does not explicitly set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute when a field becomes valid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1411: add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu.init` function registers dropdowns but does not ensure the trigger elements start with `aria-expanded="false"`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1221: add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree

issuesFound.fix: Line 1161: add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` function changes the visible state of the navigation drawer but does not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the menu has expanded.

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter._onViewToggle` function changes the grid/list view mode but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1783: add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', 'true');` (The code on line 1787 attempts this but uses `String(...)` which may not be correctly handled by all AT if the base state isn't managed).

issuesFound.title: dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter._updateCountBadge` function updates the visible product count, but there is no accompanying write to a live region to announce the new count to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1723: add `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`);`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree

issuesFound.fix: Line 1161: add `_overlay.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` function reveals the navigation drawer but does not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the menu has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1168: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter._onTabClick` function updates the active category filter but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the filter buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1746: add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', 'true');` and update the loop in `_syncTabAriaStates` (Line 1733) to use `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter._updateCountBadge` function updates the visible product count, but there is no accompanying write to an ARIA live region to announce the new count to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1714: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory));`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function applies CSS classes and updates text for validation errors but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1968: add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` in the else block.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function removes error text when a field is valid but does not explicitly set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu.init` function registers dropdowns but does not ensure the trigger elements are initialized with `aria-expanded="false"` for those that are closed on load.

issuesFound.fix: Line 1425: `.social-links a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The “Learn more” link that expands the whole card removes its outline without replacement, so keyboard users cannot see focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1065: `.feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle buttons lose their focus outline, preventing users from knowing which option is selected via keyboard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1190: `.pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .btn-sm (min‑height 20 px)
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant is only 20 px tall, below the WCAG 2.5.8 recommended 44 px target size, making it hard to tap for finger users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 285: `.btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-4); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .btn-icon (20 px × 20 px)
issuesFound.explanation: Icon‑only buttons are only 20 px square, far below the 44 px minimum, creating a difficult tap target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 312: `.btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2); }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image animates its transform on hover, but there is no reduced‑motion media query disabling this motion, which can cause discomfort for motion‑sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 720: `.hero-image img { transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower); }`  
  Line 740: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover (translateY)
issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA button moves upward on hover, creating motion without a reduced‑motion safeguard.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 770: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); box-shadow: var(--shadow-lg); transform: translateY(-1px); }`  
  Line 780: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing on body (‑0.2 em)
issuesFound.explanation: The body sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which can reduce readability, especially for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 140: `word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on a { text-decoration: none; }
issuesFound.explanation: All anchor elements have underlines removed, and no other visual differentiator (e.g., color change, border) is provided, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text for many users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 165: `a { text-decoration: underline; }`  
  (Optional: keep hover style)  
  Line 170: `a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }`

fpTitles: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria‑controls on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) controls the drawer (`_drawer`) but never receives an aria‑controls attribute pointing to the drawer’s id. Without this relationship screen readers cannot announce the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix:
After obtaining `_drawer`, ensure it has an id (e.g., `_drawer.id = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer');`) and set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id);`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑expanded on state change
issuesFound.explanation: In dropdownMenu.register the trigger button receives aria‑haspopup but never receives aria‑expanded when the dropdown opens or closes. This leaves the expanded state invisible to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In _openInstance and _closeInstance add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(inst.open));` so the attribute reflects the current state.  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion items do not update aria‑expanded on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open and close toggle the panel visibility but never modify the trigger’s aria‑expanded attribute. Users of assistive technology cannot know whether an item is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
In open(item) add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in close(item) add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels are hidden/shown without updating aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not remove aria‑hidden, and close adds hidden without setting aria‑hidden to true. The element remains in the accessibility tree when hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
In open add `item.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` and in close add `item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not update aria‑pressed when toggled
issuesFound.explanation: The period selection buttons (`_periodBtns`) receive an active class but never receive aria‑pressed to indicate their selected state. AT users cannot perceive which period is active.
issuesFound.fix:
In setPeriod after updating the UI, loop through _periodBtns and set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-period') === period));`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Billing‑toggle switch does not update aria‑pressed on state change
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off billing toggle (`_toggleEl`) is given role="switch" but never receives aria‑pressed to convey its on/off state.
issuesFound.fix:
In _ onToggleClick (and _ onToggleKeydown if needed) add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(_toggleEl.getAttribute('aria-pressed') === 'true' ? 'false' : 'true'));`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel tab buttons lack aria‑controls linking to panels
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel’s period tabs (`[data-period]` buttons) receive aria‑selected but no aria‑controls pointing to the associated panel (`[data-slide]`). AT cannot navigate from tab to panel.
issuesFound.fix:
When creating the roving‑tabindex container, assign each button an id (e.g., `btn.id = srUtils.ensureId(btn, 'carousel-tab');`) and set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', correspondingPanel.id);`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel panels are hidden/shown without updating aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Panels are hidden via the hidden attribute but never receive aria‑hidden when hidden or have it removed when shown, leaving them in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
In _ goTo after changing panel visibility, add `panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', String(!isActive));` or remove the attribute when active.  

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate marks fields as invalid visually but never adds aria‑invalid="true". Assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In _inlineValidator._renderResult when result.valid is false, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid when field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, inlineValidator._renderResult removes visual error classes but does not remove aria‑invalid. The invalid state persists for AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In the same function, when result.valid is true, add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile drawer is closed, the code adds/removes CSS classes but never sets aria‑hidden on the drawer element, so it remains perceivable to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.close add `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` and in mobileNav.open add `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');`.

fpTitles: Testimonials carousel panels are hidden/shown without updating aria‑hidden
Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid when field becomes valid
Mobile navigation drawer hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle functions do not update aria‑expanded on the trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions in mobileNav change CSS classes and scroll‑lock but never set aria‑expanded on the hamburger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'true') in open() and setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false') in close().

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑expanded initialisation on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The navToggle button is queried into _trigger but no aria‑expanded attribute is set initially, so its collapsed state is not conveyed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init after _trigger is obtained, add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls reference to the drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens the mobile menu does not have an aria‑controls attribute pointing to the drawer element, breaking the relationship between control and controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: After _trigger and _ drawer are set, add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑controls', _drawer.id || 'nav-drawer').

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers do not set aria‑expanded when opening/closing panels
issuesFound.explanation: In dropdownMenu.register the trigger button receives aria‑haspopup but never receives aria‑expanded updates, so screen‑reader users cannot tell whether the submenu is open.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance set setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'true') and in _closeInstance set setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger buttons are not given an initial aria‑expanded="false", leaving their collapsed state ambiguous.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.init or register after creating inst, add setAttr(trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button does not update aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The element with [data‑billing‑toggle] acts as a two‑state switch but the code never sets aria‑pressed, so assistive technologies cannot perceive its state.
issuesFound.fix: In _​onToggleClick or the click handler, after toggling the period, call setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria‑pressed', _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false').

issuesFound.title: Search combobox input does not set aria‑expanded when suggestion list is shown/hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The search <input> is given role="combobox" and aria‑controls but never receives aria‑expanded, so screen‑reader users cannot know when the listbox is open.
issuesFound.fix: In siteSearch._open add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria‑expanded', 'true'); and in siteSearch._close set 'false'.

issuesFound.title: Search combobox input missing initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The search <input> does not have an aria‑expanded attribute initially, leaving its default state undefined.
issuesFound.fix: After _​inputEl is obtained in siteSearch.init, add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria‑expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate adds error messages but never adds aria‑invalid="true" to the offending input, so AT does not know the field is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator.validate after a failed validateField, call setAttr(field, 'aria‑invalid', 'true') (and optionally setAttr(field, 'aria‑describedby', errorEl.id)).

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not clear aria‑invalid when field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field becomes valid, the code removes the error message but does not remove aria‑invalid, leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator.validate when result.valid is true, call removeAttr(field, 'aria‑invalid') (or setAttr(field, 'aria‑invalid', 'false')).

fpTitles: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls reference to the drawer
Dropdown menu triggers missing initial aria‑expanded state on page load
Billing‑period toggle button does not update aria‑pressed state
Search combobox input missing initial aria‑expanded state on page load

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria‑controls link to drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button does not reference the navigation drawer via aria‑controls, so the relationship between control and controlled region is unclear.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an ID to the drawer (e.g., `id="nav-drawer"`) and set on the trigger: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', 'nav-drawer');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑expanded when opened/closed
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button receives no aria‑expanded state changes, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu’s visibility.
issuesFound.fix:
In the open path call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in the close path call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Search input does not update aria‑expanded when suggestion list opens
issuesFound.explanation: The search `<input>` receives aria‑autocomplete and aria‑controls but never receives aria‑expanded when the suggestion panel becomes visible, breaking the combobox pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
When opening the suggestion list (`_open()`), add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; when closing, set to `'false'`.  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger does not update aria‑expanded on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items lack aria‑expanded state changes on their toggle buttons, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which items are expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
In `open(item)` call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `close(item)` call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button hidden without aria‑hidden state updates
issuesFound.explanation: The button is shown/hidden via the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but when hidden it is not marked aria‑hidden="true", leaving it in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
When hiding, add `setAttr(_btn, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; when showing, remove the attribute or set to `'false'`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hide/show does not update aria‑hidden on the <header> element
issuesFound.explanation: The header is hidden by adding a CSS class, but its aria‑hidden state is never toggled, so screen‑reader users may still navigate to it when it is visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
When hiding, `setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; when showing, `removeAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden')`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Simple billing toggle switch (data‑billing‑toggle) missing aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off switch toggles visual state but never updates aria‑pressed, breaking the ARIA switch pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_onToggleClick`, after toggling, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', _toggleEl.getAttribute('aria-pressed') === 'true' ? 'false' : 'true')`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates in product filter do not announce via live region
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the count badge is updated but no ARIA live region is notified, so users are unaware that the product list changed.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the count, call `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} shown`);`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Toast notifications lack aria‑live assertive for error toasts
issuesFound.explanation: The toast container uses aria‑live="polite". Error toasts should be announced assertively to ensure timely awareness.
issuesFound.fix:
When creating an error toast (`type === 'error'`), set `setAttr(toast, 'aria-live', 'assertive')` instead of the default.  

---

issuesFound.title: File‑upload zone does not set aria‑hidden when hidden (e.g., after successful upload)
issuesFound.explanation: After a file finishes uploading, the entry remains in the DOM without updating aria‑hidden, potentially confusing screen‑reader navigation.
issuesFound.fix: On successful upload, add `setAttr(entry.rowEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true')` or remove the entry from the list.

fpTitles: FAQ accordion trigger does not update aria‑expanded on open/close
Sticky header hide/show does not update aria‑hidden on the <header> element
File‑upload zone does not set aria‑hidden when hidden (e.g., after successful upload)

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `<button>` that toggles the navigation dropdown does not have an `aria-expanded` attribute, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the submenu is open or closed. This affects all users of the primary navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 274: `<button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile menu toggle `<button>` lacks an `aria-expanded` attribute, leaving assistive technology unaware of the menu’s open/closed state. This impacts keyboard and screen‑reader users on mobile.
issuesFound.fix: Line 447: `<button id={toggleId} ref={menuToggleRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-label={isOpen ? 'Close navigation menu' : 'Open navigation menu'} className="mobile-nav-toggle" onClick={isOpen ? close : open}>`

issuesFound.title: IconButton in MobileNav close missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The `<IconButton>` used to close the mobile navigation panel contains only an icon (`✕`) and does not provide an `aria-label`, leaving the button nameless to assistive technologies. This hinders users trying to close the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 418: `<IconButton onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close" aria-label="Close navigation panel">✕</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: IconButton in ThemeToggle missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle button renders only an icon (sun/moon/computer) without an `aria-label`, so screen‑reader users receive no description of its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 527: `<IconButton onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle" aria-label={LABELS[theme]}>`

issuesFound.title: IconButton (prev) in TestimonialsCarousel missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The previous‑slide control is an `<IconButton>` that contains only the “‹” character and lacks an `aria-label`, making it inaccessible to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1012: `<IconButton aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev" aria-label="Previous testimonial">‹</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: IconButton (next) in TestimonialsCarousel missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The next‑slide control is an `<IconButton>` that contains only the “›” character and lacks an `aria-label`, preventing screen‑reader users from understanding its function.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1018: `<IconButton aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next" aria-label="Next testimonial">›</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label not associated with input (NewsletterForm)
issuesFound.explanation: `<FormGroup>` renders a `<label>` without an `htmlFor` attribute, so the email `<TextInput>` inside is not programmatically associated with its label. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 for form controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 862: `<label htmlFor="newsletter-email" className="form-label">Email address</label>` (and ensure the `FormGroup` component uses the `htmlFor` prop when rendering the label).

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid when in error state
issuesFound.explanation: The `<TextInput>` does not forward an `aria-invalid` attribute even when the `invalid` prop is true, so assistive technologies cannot announce validation errors. This affects all form fields that become invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 730: `<input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: Required‑field visual marker not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk (`*`) indicating a required field is rendered as visible text without `aria-hidden="true"`, causing screen readers to read the stray asterisk.
issuesFound.fix: Line 845: ` *`

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed to convey selection
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly/annual billing buttons indicate the selected period visually but lack `aria-pressed`, leaving AT users unaware of the current selection.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 945 (monthly button): `<button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={...} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>Monthly</button>`  
  Fix: Line 951 (annual button): `<button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={...} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>Annual <Badge variant="success">Save 20%</Badge></button>`

issuesFound.title: Navigation links missing aria-current="page" for current page
issuesFound.explanation: The primary navigation marks the current page with `item.current` and a CSS class but does not set `aria-current="page"`, so screen‑reader users cannot identify the active page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 332 (inside DesktopNav): `{item.label}`

issuesFound.title: Button loading state not communicated via aria-busy (Button component)
issuesFound.explanation: The `<Button>` component accepts an `isLoading` prop but does not set `aria-busy`, so assistive technologies are not informed that the button is busy. This affects all loading buttons (e.g., newsletter submit).
issuesFound.fix: Line 558 (inside Button return): `<button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={...} {...rest}>`

issuesFound.title: Spinner component missing aria-hidden (decorative)
issuesFound.explanation: The `<Spinner>` renders visual spinner elements but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the decorative parts, causing screen readers to announce unnecessary content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 587: `<span role="status" aria-label={label} aria-hidden="true" className={`spinner spinner--${size}`} aria-live="polite">`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor
issuesFound.explanation: The <label> rendered by FormGroup does not include an htmlFor attribute, so screen readers cannot associate the label with its input. This affects all form fields that use FormGroup, preventing users from understanding the purpose of the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: <label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">

issuesFound.title: Icon‑only button missing accessible name (ThemeToggle)
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used for the theme toggle contains only an icon and does not provide an aria-label, so screen‑reader users have no indication of its purpose. This affects all users trying to change the theme.
issuesFound.fix: Line 540: <IconButton aria-label="Toggle theme" onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">

issuesFound.title: Icon‑only button missing accessible name (MobileNav close)
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used to close the mobile navigation panel contains only an icon and lacks an aria-label, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of its function.
issuesFound.fix: Line 460: <IconButton aria-label="Close mobile navigation" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">✕</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: Icon‑only button missing accessible name (Testimonials carousel previous)
issuesFound.explanation: The previous‑slide IconButton in the testimonials carousel contains only an icon and has no aria-label, so screen‑reader users cannot navigate the carousel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1150: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">‹</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: Icon‑only button missing accessible name (Testimonials carousel next)
issuesFound.explanation: The next‑slide IconButton in the testimonials carousel contains only an icon and has no aria-label, so screen‑reader users cannot navigate the carousel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1156: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">›</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: Disclosure trigger missing aria‑expanded (Desktop navigation dropdown)
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens a navigation submenu does not have aria‑expanded, so assistive technologies cannot convey whether the submenu is open. This affects keyboard users navigating the desktop menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 380: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: Disclosure trigger missing aria‑expanded (Mobile navigation panel toggle)

issuesFound.title: Disclosure trigger missing aria‑expanded (Mobile navigation submenu toggle – Products)
issuesFound.explanation: The button that expands the “Products” submenu in the mobile nav does not have aria‑expanded, so its state is not announced.

issuesFound.title: Disclosure trigger missing aria‑expanded (Mobile navigation submenu toggle – Solutions)
issuesFound.explanation: The button that expands the “Solutions” submenu in the mobile nav does not have aria‑expanded, so its state is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 572: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: Disclosure trigger missing aria‑expanded (Mobile navigation submenu toggle – Resources)
issuesFound.explanation: The button that expands the “Resources” submenu in the mobile nav does not have aria‑expanded, so its state is not announced.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑pressed (Billing period toggle)
issuesFound.explanation: The buttons that switch between “Monthly” and “Annual” billing do not have aria‑pressed, so the selected state is not conveyed to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1020: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} className={...} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>Monthly</button>
  Fix: Line 1026: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} className={...} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>Annual <Badge variant="success">Save 20%</Badge></button>

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑pressed (Filter tabs)
issuesFound.explanation: The filter‑tab buttons do not have aria‑pressed, so the active filter is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 880: <button type="button" aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} className={...} tabIndex={...} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all elements that do not have a more specific focus rule, making keyboard navigation impossible to track.

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none and outline-offset but does not define a visible outline color, width, box-shadow, or border. Keyboard users cannot identify which button is focused.

issuesFound.title: Header logo link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible rule removes the outline without adding a box-shadow or border. This hides focus state for the logo link.

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when the mobile menu button is focused.

issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible rule removes the outline without a visible alternative. Focus is invisible on dropdown links.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab focus indicator removed without replacement

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Focus is invisible on billing toggle buttons.

issuesFound.title: Table wrapper focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .table-wrapper:focus-visible rule removes the outline without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see focus on the scrollable table region.

issuesFound.title: FAQ button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Focus is invisible on accordion headers.

issuesFound.title: Footer brand link focus indicator removed without replacement

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation link focus indicator removed without replacement

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility renders content visibly
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and clip: auto, which makes content fully visible instead of hiding it visually for screen readers only. This breaks layout and exposes helper text to sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on section header paragraph
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p text uses #aaaaaa on a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approx 2.8:1. This fails WCAG AA (4.5:1) and is hard to read for low-vision users.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on feature card text
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card p text uses #aaaaaa on a white background, failing WCAG AA contrast requirements (2.8:1).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on step content text
issuesFound.explanation: The .step-content p text uses #aaaaaa on a white background, failing WCAG AA contrast requirements.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on product body text
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-body > p text uses #aaaaaa on a white background, failing WCAG AA contrast requirements.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on testimonial caption text
issuesFound.explanation: The .testimonial-card figcaption p text uses #aaaaaa on a white background, failing WCAG AA contrast requirements.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on input hint text
issuesFound.explanation: The .input-hint text uses #aaaaaa on a white background, failing WCAG AA contrast requirements.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on contact section text
issuesFound.explanation: The .contact-section > p text uses #aaaaaa on a light background, failing WCAG AA contrast requirements.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a text uses #94a3b8 on a #555555 background. Contrast is approx 2.5:1, failing WCAG AA.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer bottom text
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-bottom > p text uses #64748b on a #555555 background. Contrast is approx 1.3:1, failing WCAG AA severely.

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target too small
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24px (SC 2.5.8).

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target too small
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height to 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24px.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox touch target too small
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] sets width and height to 20px, below the 24px minimum target size.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transition: transform, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 855 is empty and does not disable this motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing can cause letters to overlap or words to become illegible for users with cognitive or visual disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Base font size below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px. Since body uses 1rem, all body text renders at 12px, which is below the recommended 16px baseline for readability.

issuesFound.title: Forced-colors mode removes focus outline
issuesFound.explanation: Inside @media (forced-colors: active), the :focus-visible rule sets outline: none. This removes the browser's high-contrast focus indicator, making navigation impossible in Windows High Contrast Mode.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on hover
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a links have text-decoration: none and only change color on hover. Color alone is not sufficient to indicate interactive state (SC 1.4.1).

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This removes the keyboard focus ring for all interactive elements, making it impossible for keyboard users to identify which element is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 307: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which condenses text and reduces readability for users with cognitive disabilities or dyslexia.
issuesFound.fix: Line 259: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have text-decoration: none and rely solely on colour change on hover to indicate interactivity. Users with colour blindness may not identify them as links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1091: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: underline; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links on .social-links a

issuesFound.title: keyboardNavigationGuard references undefined SUPPORTS.dom property

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator references undefined SUPPORTS.reducedMotion property

issuesFound.title: prefetchManager references undefined SUPPORTS.prefetch property

issuesFound.title: mediaPlayerController references undefined SUPPORTS.reducedMotion property

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class incorrectly implemented
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide content and makes it visible in the layout while keeping it in the accessibility tree, or potentially removes it from the tree depending on browser. It should use position: absolute and clipping.

issuesFound.title: Featured pricing card transform lacks reduced motion override

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing on headings impairs readability

issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) and color: var(--colour-text-on-brand) (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.7:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (requires 4.5:1).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on primary button

issuesFound.title: Root font size below 16px reduces readability
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px, which is below the 16px (1rem) recommended minimum for body text, forcing users to zoom to read comfortably and potentially breaking user font preferences.

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed globally without consistent replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. While some components add underlines on hover, others (like footer navigation) do not provide a non-colour visual differentiator, making it difficult to identify links among text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 323: Remove the rule or add: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` or ensure all link components have visible underlines or borders on hover/focus. For minimal change: `a { text-decoration: none; } a:hover, a:focus { text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none and outline-offset without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus indicator for all interactive elements across the site, making keyboard navigation impossible for sighted users.
issuesFound.fix: Add a visible focus indicator such as box-shadow or outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); to the :focus-visible rule.

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector explicitly sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the visible focus ring from buttons. No alternative visual indicator such as box-shadow or border-color is provided in the same rule.
issuesFound.fix: Add visible focus styles: ".btn:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }"

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the outline in focus-visible state with only outline-offset specified. This leaves no visible indicator when the button receives keyboard focus.
issuesFound.fix: Add replacement focus styles: ".nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }"

issuesFound.title: Header logo link focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link in the header removes the outline on focus-visible without providing an alternative visual indicator, making it impossible to see when the link has keyboard focus.
issuesFound.fix: Add replacement focus styles: ".header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }"

issuesFound.title: Primary navigation links focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links and buttons remove the outline on focus-visible state with no box-shadow or border replacement. Users cannot see which navigation item is currently focused.
issuesFound.fix: Add visible focus indicator: "#nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible, #nav-menu > li > button:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }"

issuesFound.title: Submenu links focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links set outline: none with outline-offset: -3px but provide no visible alternative focus indicator such as background-color change or box-shadow.
issuesFound.fix: Add replacement focus styles: "[id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }"

issuesFound.title: Search submit button focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The search form submit button removes outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset specified, leaving no visible indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Add replacement focus styles: "#site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }"

issuesFound.title: Account navigation links focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: Links in the account navigation remove the outline on focus-visible without providing a visible alternative, making keyboard navigation invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Add replacement focus styles: "nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }"

issuesFound.title: Feature card links focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The "Learn more" links in feature cards remove outline on focus-visible with no replacement visual indicator, despite extending the click area via pseudo-element.
issuesFound.fix: Add replacement focus styles: ".feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }"

issuesFound.title: Filter tabs focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter toggle buttons remove outline on focus-visible state without providing an alternative visible indicator such as box-shadow or border-color change.
issuesFound.fix: Add replacement focus styles: ".filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }"

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle buttons focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: Monthly/annual pricing toggle buttons remove outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Add replacement focus styles: ".pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }"

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion buttons focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion toggle buttons remove outline on focus-visible with outline-offset: -3px but provide no visible alternative focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Add replacement focus styles: ".faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }"

issuesFound.title: Checkbox focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: Custom checkbox inputs remove outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset specified, making it impossible to see when the checkbox has keyboard focus.
issuesFound.fix: Add replacement focus styles: ".form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }"

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation links focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove outline on focus-visible without providing a visible alternative indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Add replacement focus styles: "footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }"

issuesFound.title: Footer brand links focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: Links within the footer brand section remove outline on focus-visible with no replacement visual indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Add replacement focus styles: ".footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }"

issuesFound.title: Social links focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove outline on focus-visible without providing a visible alternative, making keyboard navigation invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Add replacement focus styles: ".social-links a:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }"

issuesFound.title: Table wrapper focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The table scrollable region and focus-ring-inset utility class remove outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset specified, providing no visible focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Add replacement focus styles: ".table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }" and ".focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }"

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate these buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Increase minimum size: ".btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); }"

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons set min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target size. This creates a barrier for users with dexterity limitations.
issuesFound.fix: Increase minimum size: ".btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); }"

issuesFound.title: Checkbox touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: Custom checkboxes explicitly set width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. This makes the checkbox difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Increase size: ".form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; }"

issuesFound.title: Standard button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: Standard buttons set min-height: 32px, which meets the 24px minimum but falls below the 44px recommended target size for AAA compliance. Users with motor impairments may accidentally activate adjacent elements.
issuesFound.fix: Increase minimum height: ".btn { min-height: 44px; }"

issuesFound.title: Large button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: Large buttons set min-height: 36px, which is below the 44px recommended target size and may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate reliably.
issuesFound.fix: Increase minimum height: ".btn-lg { min-height: 44px; }"

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px, below the 44px recommended target size, making it difficult to activate on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Increase minimum size: ".nav-toggle { min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; }"

issuesFound.title: Navigation menu items touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links and buttons set min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size, creating a potential barrier for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Increase minimum height: "#nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { min-height: 44px; }"

issuesFound.title: Submenu links touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links set min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended target size.
issuesFound.fix: Increase minimum height: "[id$="-submenu"] li a { min-height: 44px; }"

issuesFound.title: Search input touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The search input sets height: 28px, below the 44px recommended target size for touch inputs.
issuesFound.fix: Increase height: "#search-input { height: 44px; }"

issuesFound.title: Search submit button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets width: 28px and height: 28px, below the 44px recommended target size.
issuesFound.fix: Increase size: "#site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; }"

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons set min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended target size.
issuesFound.fix: Increase minimum height: ".filter-tabs button { min-height: 44px; }"

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle buttons touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons set min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended target size.
issuesFound.fix: Increase minimum height: ".pricing-toggle button { min-height: 44px; }"

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation links touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set min-height: 24px, meeting the minimum but below the 44px recommended target size.
issuesFound.fix: Increase minimum height: "footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { min-height: 44px; }"

issuesFound.title: Social links touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links set min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended target size.
issuesFound.fix: Increase minimum height: ".social-links a { min-height: 44px; }"

issuesFound.title: Screen reader utility class broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which makes the content visible instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This defeats the purpose of the utility.
issuesFound.fix: Replace with correct visually hidden styles: ".sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }"

issuesFound.title: Button motion transition missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons include transform in their transition properties (transform var(--transition-fast)), which causes spatial movement, but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override is provided to disable this animation for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Add reduced motion override: "@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }"

issuesFound.title: Hero image motion transition missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: Hero images transition transform and box-shadow properties, creating spatial movement on hover, but lack a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable these effects for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: Add reduced motion override: "@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } }"

issuesFound.title: Feature card motion transition missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: Feature cards transition transform properties (translateY), creating spatial movement on hover, without a prefers-reduced-motion override to respect user motion preferences.
issuesFound.fix: Add to existing media query: "@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card { transition: border-color var(--transition-base), box-shadow var(--transition-base); } }"

issuesFound.title: Feature card link arrow motion missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The arrow pseudo-element transitions transform (translateX), creating horizontal movement, without a complete reduced motion override (only transform is disabled but transition remains).
issuesFound.fix: Ensure complete override: "@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card a::after { transition: none; } }"

issuesFound.title: Product card motion transition missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards transition transform properties (translateY), creating spatial movement on hover, without a prefers-reduced-motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Add to existing media query: "@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base); } }"

issuesFound.title: Pricing card motion transition missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing cards transition transform properties (translateY, scale), creating spatial movement on hover, without a prefers-reduced-motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Add to existing media query: "@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base); } }"

issuesFound.title: Stat item motion transition missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: Stat items transition transform properties (translateY), creating spatial movement on hover, without a prefers-reduced-motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Add to existing media query: "@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .stat-item { transition: background-color var(--transition-base); } }"

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion icon motion missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion toggle icon transitions transform (rotation), creating rotational movement, but the reduced motion media query only disables the transition property without providing an instant state change alternative.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure complete override: "@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .faq-item dt button::after { transition: none; transform: none; } }"

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Remove negative spacing: "body { word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }" or set to 0.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on statistics labels
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics section uses color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) (30% opacity white) against a gradient background of approximately #5588bb to #bb88ee. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.1:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Increase opacity: ".stat-label { color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85); }"

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without alternative visual indicator
issuesFound.explanation: The global anchor element selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. No alternative visual differentiator such as border-bottom, font-weight change, or background highlight is provided as a default, making links distinguishable only by color.
issuesFound.fix: Add underline on hover/focus or default underline: "a { text-decoration: underline; }" or "a:hover, a:focus { text-decoration: underline; }" combined with distinct color.
