issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions show and hide dropdown panels but do not set aria-expanded="true" or "false" on the trigger buttons. Assistive technologies cannot announce the expanded state of the menus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2105 (in _openInstance): Add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); Line 2120 (in _closeInstance): Add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open function adds CSS classes and removes the hidden attribute from the panel, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Although the close function sets aria-expanded="false", the asymmetry leaves screen readers unaware when items are expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3422 (after item.open = true): Add setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: Search combobox does not toggle aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _open and _close functions manage the visibility of the suggestion listbox but never update aria-expanded on the search input element. Screen reader users cannot tell when suggestions are available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2755 (in _open): Add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); Line 2765 (in _close): Add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons indicate active state via CSS classes but do not set aria-pressed. Since these buttons act as toggles that filter the product grid, screen readers need the pressed state to convey which filter is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3055 (inside forEach): Add setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function adds error descriptions via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields. Conversely, markValid removes describedby references but does not clear aria-invalid. Screen readers cannot identify which fields have failed validation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1205 (in markInvalid): Add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true'); Line 1215 (in markValid): Add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is associated with a drawer panel but the init function does not set aria-controls to reference the drawer's ID. Screen readers cannot establish the programmatic relationship between the button and the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1825 (after _drawer assignment): Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is closed on page load but the trigger button is not initialised with aria-expanded="false". Screen readers announce the button without indicating that it controls a collapsed menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1826 (after _trigger assignment): Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: Search combobox missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is assigned role="combobox" and aria-controls pointing to the listbox, but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false". Screen readers cannot convey that the suggestions are initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2560 (after setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox')): Add setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Product filter changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count updates in the DOM but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed that the product list has been filtered or how many results are now showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3010 (after _updateCountBadge call in applyFilters): Add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);

issuesFound.title: Pricing period changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates price displays but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are unaware that prices have changed or which billing period is now active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3235 (after _syncToggleState(period)): Add liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items emits custom DOM events but does not announce the state change to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technologies cannot tell when an answer has been revealed or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3428 (in open): Add liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`); Line 3445 (in close): Add liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav drawer not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The close function removes CSS classes to hide the drawer but relies solely on CSS display:none. If the CSS uses other hiding methods (off-screen positioning), the drawer remains in the accessibility tree. The function should explicitly set aria-hidden="true" when closing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1875 (after removeClass calls): Add setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true'); Line 1860 (in open): Add setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');

fpTitles: FAQ accordion changes not announced

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on question buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion items are toggled via trigger buttons, but the open(item) and close(item) functions only animate the panel height and toggle CSS classes. They fail to synchronise the aria-expanded attribute on item.trigger, leaving screen readers unaware of the current expanded/collapsed state after user interaction.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2834 (in faqAccordion.open() after addClass(item.el, CSS.open)): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 2856 (in faqAccordion.close() after removeClass(item.el, CSS.open)): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _open/_close functions do not update aria-expanded on combobox input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox that shows/hides a suggestion listbox, but the _open() and _close() functions never toggle aria-expanded on the input element. Assistive technologies cannot announce whether the suggestions are currently visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1962 (in siteSearch._open() after _searchState.open = true): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 1975 (in siteSearch._close() after _searchState.open = false): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period buttons (monthly/quarterly/annual) function as toggle buttons indicating the active selection, but _syncToggleState() only toggles CSS classes. The aria-pressed attribute is never updated to reflect which period is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2460 (inside _periodBtns.forEach in _syncToggleState): Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce results to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count and empty state are updated visually, but there is no live-region announcement. Screen reader users are not informed that the product list has changed or how many results are now showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2140 (in applyFilters() after _updateCountBadge(totalMatches)): Add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products match your filters`);` (or use STRINGS.filterApplied if available)

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button does not announce action to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the scroll-to-top button is activated, the page scrolls to the top and focus moves to the skip link, but no announcement is made via the live region to confirm the action for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1418 (in scrollToTop._onClick() after window.scrollTo): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce billing period change
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched (monthly/annual), the price display updates visually, but the change is not announced to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2428 (in pricing.setPeriod() after _updateSavingsMessage(period)): Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Billing changed to {period}').replace('{period}', BILLING_PERIODS[period].label));`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on validated fields
issuesFound.explanation: The validation renderer marks fields as valid or invalid visually and updates aria-describedby, but never sets the aria-invalid attribute. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state of form fields to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3145 (in _renderResult valid branch after srUtils.markValid): Add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`
  Line 3150 (in _renderResult invalid branch after srUtils.markInvalid): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: During initialization, the mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded set to "false" to indicate that the drawer is initially closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 768 (in mobileNav.init() after _trigger is assigned): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search input element acts as a combobox, but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" when the suggestion listbox is hidden on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2025 (in siteSearch.init() after _inputEl is assigned): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown menu, the trigger button has aria-haspopup and aria-controls ensured, but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" even though the panel is hidden by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 872 (in dropdownMenu.register() after panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')): Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner not removed from accessibility tree during fade out
issuesFound.explanation: When hiding the cookie consent banner, the fadeOut animation is triggered but aria-hidden is not set to "true" first. The banner remains in the accessibility tree during the fade-out transition, allowing screen readers to interact with disappearing content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3915 (in cookieConsent._hideBanner() at the start of the function): Add `setAttr(_bannerEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` before the fadeOut call.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked state
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle element is assigned role="switch", but the aria-checked attribute is never set to reflect the on/off state (annual vs monthly). Screen readers cannot convey the switch state to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2495 (in pricing.init() after setting role="switch"): Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));`
  Line 2510 (in pricing._onToggleClick() after setPeriod(next)): Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(next === 'year'));`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button controls the drawer panel, but the aria-controls attribute is not set to reference the drawer's ID, preventing screen readers from identifying the controlled element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 768 (in mobileNav.init() after _drawer is assigned): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));` (ensuring the drawer has an ID first)

fpTitles: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls attribute

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, the code ensures aria-controls and aria-haspopup are present, but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" for triggers that are closed by default. Screen readers may not recognise these as expandable controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2875: After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `if (!trigger.hasAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden toggle
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile drawer is closed, it remains in the accessibility tree because the code does not set aria-hidden="true". When opened, aria-hidden is not removed. This causes screen readers to access hidden content or miss visible content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2655: Add `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` in open function. Line 2670: Add `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` in close function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing initial aria-expanded and aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function locates the trigger button (_trigger) and drawer (_drawer) but does not establish the accessibility relationship: it omits initial aria-expanded="false" on the trigger and aria-controls linking the trigger to the drawer.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2690: After `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add:
  `if (_trigger) { if (!_trigger.hasAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); if (_drawer && !_trigger.getAttribute('aria-controls')) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav')); }`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch initialisation configures the input as a combobox with aria-haspopup and aria-controls, but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" state required for collapsed comboboxes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3580: After `suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);`, add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period buttons (_periodBtns) act as a toggle group where one button is always pressed, but the code only toggles CSS classes. It does not set aria-pressed to convey the pressed state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4250: Inside the forEach loop, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked invalid, the function updates aria-describedby to link the error message, but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1920: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` at the beginning of the markInvalid function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid cleanup
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing the invalid state, the function removes the aria-describedby reference but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute, leaving stale invalid state that screen readers may still report.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1930: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` after updating describedby.

issuesFound.title: pricing price changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch billing periods, the price text content changes dynamically, but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are unaware of the price update.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4210: After `planDataParser.getAll().forEach(plan => _updatePlanCard(plan, period));`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter results not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count updates, but this change is not announced via a live region, so screen reader users do not receive feedback on the filter results.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3840: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu focus not returned to trigger on close
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is closed via the Escape key, the returnFocus parameter is accepted but never used to move focus back to the trigger button. Keyboard users are stranded at the closed panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2855: At the end of _closeInstance, add `if (returnFocus && inst.trigger) inst.trigger.focus();`

issuesFound.title: newsletter email missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form email input is queried but not programmatically assigned autocomplete="email", preventing password managers and browsers from autofill.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4550: After `_emailEl = qs('input[type="email"]', _formEl);`, add `if (_emailEl) _emailEl.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: contactForm email missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form email field (selector '[name="email"]') is validated but the code does not set autocomplete="email" on this personal information input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4720: In the init function where fields are bound, after finding the email field, add `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: contactForm full name missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form full name field (selector '[name="full_name"]') collects personal information but lacks autocomplete="name", hindering autofill.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4720: After binding the full_name field, add `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button in SiteHeader manages an open/closed state but lacks aria-expanded. Assistive technology users cannot tell whether the mobile navigation panel is currently visible or hidden.

issuesFound.title: Theme toggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle component renders an IconButton without an aria-label prop, violating the component's required interface and leaving the button without an accessible name. Screen reader users hear only "button" with no indication of its purpose.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel previous button is an IconButton rendered without an aria-label prop. As an icon-only button, it lacks any text content or accessible name, leaving screen reader users unable to determine its function.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel next button is an IconButton rendered without an aria-label prop. As an icon-only button with no text content, screen reader users cannot identify its purpose or destination.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation backdrop div missing interactive semantics and keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation backdrop is a div element with an onClick handler but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handling. Keyboard users cannot focus or activate the backdrop to close the navigation using Enter or Space keys.

issuesFound.title: Modal backdrop div missing interactive semantics and keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay div handles click events to close the dialog but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers. Keyboard-only users cannot dismiss the modal by activating the backdrop.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2104: <div className="modal-overlay" aria-modal="true" onClick={onClose} role="button" tabIndex={-1} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }} aria-label="Close dialog">

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid propagation
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop to style error states but does not map it to aria-invalid. Screen reader users cannot detect when the input contains invalid data.

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid propagation
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop for styling error states but fails to propagate this state to aria-invalid. Assistive technology cannot convey the invalid state to users.

issuesFound.title: Select component missing aria-invalid propagation
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component accepts an invalid prop for visual error styling but does not set aria-invalid on the select element. Screen reader users are not notified when the selection is invalid.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Checkbox component accepts an invalid prop but does not apply aria-invalid to the input element. Screen reader users cannot detect validation errors on checkboxes.

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component supports an isLoading state that disables the button and shows a spinner, but it lacks aria-busy. Screen reader users are not informed that the button is actively processing a submission.

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav component renders a nav element without an aria-label or aria-labelledby. Since multiple nav elements exist on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish this primary navigation from others.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1179: <nav id="main-nav" className="desktop-nav" aria-label="Main navigation">

issuesFound.title: Skip navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component contains a nav element without an accessible name. Without aria-label, screen reader users cannot identify this as the skip navigation region distinct from other navigation landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 595: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan card article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The PlanCard component renders an article element without aria-labelledby. Because multiple pricing cards appear together, screen reader users cannot identify which plan each card represents when navigating by landmark.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1780: <article className="pricing-card__article" aria-labelledby={headingId}>

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active link in DesktopNav uses a CSS class to indicate the current page but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot identify which page is currently active in the navigation.

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog missing aria-modal attribute on dialog element
issuesFound.explanation: The Modal component places aria-modal="true" on the overlay div instead of the dialog element itself. Screen reader users may not recognize the dialog as modal, potentially allowing navigation outside the dialog boundaries.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation dialog missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The MobileNav dialog has role="dialog" but lacks aria-modal="true". Without this attribute, screen reader users may not understand that the panel is modal and that interaction is confined within it.

issuesFound.title: Statistic value not announcing formatted label to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The StatItem component renders statistical values like "40,000+" and "4.2M" without using the provided valueLabel (e.g., "40,000 plus", "4.2 million"). Screen readers may mispronounce these values or read symbols literally, confusing users.

issuesFound.title: Integration bar duplicate list not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The IntegrationsBar renders a duplicate list of integration logos for visual looping effect, but the list lacks aria-hidden="true". Screen reader users encounter the same list of partners twice, causing unnecessary repetition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1483: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: Required field marker asterisk not hidden from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component renders a visual asterisk (*) to indicate required fields without aria-hidden="true". Screen readers announce "star" or "asterisk" before announcing the "(required)" text, creating redundant and confusing output.

issuesFound.title: Star rating symbols not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The StarRating component renders star symbols (★ and ☆) as text content inside spans without aria-hidden. Although the parent has an aria-label, some screen readers may still announce the star characters individually in addition to the label, creating verbose output.

fpTitles: Checkbox component missing aria-invalid attribute
Modal dialog missing aria-modal attribute on dialog element
Mobile navigation dialog missing aria-modal attribute

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active link missing aria-current

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron icon not hidden from assistive technology (Solutions)

issuesFound.title: StarRating visual stars not hidden from assistive technology

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article element missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog aria-modal attribute misplaced
issuesFound.explanation: The modal's aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the backdrop overlay div instead of the dialog div with role="dialog", causing assistive technology to treat the entire page (including the backdrop) as modal rather than just the dialog content, potentially trapping focus incorrectly or confusing the user about the modal boundary.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav dialog missing aria-modal attribute

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel non-visible slide missing aria-hidden
